title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Inference Brief: Esm Cjs Resolution
summary: The service reads configuration during requests, starts background promises without ownership, mixes module systems, treats signals as ordinary exceptions, and reports ready before dependencies exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts esm cjs resolution increment in process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.
Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Esm Cjs Resolution · Theory Esm Cjs Resolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4680 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Esm Cjs Resolution contract for the supplied data.
